items.title: Oil Industry Retained Up to €200 Million of German Fuel Subsidy

items.title: Spain’s Joint Debt Proposal Questioned by EU’s Largest Economies
items.description: A Spanish bid to boost joint debt issuance by the European Union was met with skepticism from some of the bloc’s top economies.

items.title: Franklin’s Dudley Sees AI Infrastructure Bull Case Through 2027

items.title: ING Weighs Venezuela Commodity Deals as Client Requests Rise
items.description: ING Groep, one of the top financiers of commodity trading, is fielding “a lot” of calls from clients to support Venezuelan natural resource deals as the country revamps exports after years of US sanct

items.title: Why Fed Cuts Beat Rate Hikes
items.description: Markets may be pricing yesterday's economy. iCapital's Dan Suzuki joined Bloomberg Open Interest to explain why slowing job growth and cooling inflation make Fed rate cuts more likely than hikes, why

items.title: Wall Street Finds a New Way to Help Investors Avoid Elon Musk
items.description: For every investor who wants more Elon Musk, Wall Street increasingly has an ETF. Now it’s planning some for investors who want less.

items.title: A New Texas Mayor Takes Over Amid 2,200% Tax-Base Boom
items.description: Mark Hill, the new mayor of Frisco, Texas, talks about the challenges and opportunities of managing a city whose population has soared soared 600% and whose tax base is up 2,200% in the past 25 years.

items.title: UK Fintech Lendable Pledges Personal Loans to Raise £500 Million
items.description: UK digital lender Lendable Ltd. is raising £500 million ($671 million) by selling securities backed by a portfolio of personal loans, a deal that would support its ambitions to push further into lendi

items.title: Corn, Soy Ease Ahead of WASDE as Traders Eye Relief From Heat
items.description: Corn and soybean futures were easing on pressure from an improving US weather outlook as traders looked ahead to the monthly WASDE supply and demand outlook due Friday.

items.title: Tech Drives US Stocks Higher as Traders Downplay Iran War Risks
items.description: US stocks rose as investors put aside concerns over the latest flare-up between the US and Iran to pile on technology stocks after recent declines spurred dip buying.

items.title: MiniMax Seeks $1.9 Billion From Share Sale, Convertible Bond
items.description: MiniMax Group Inc. is looking to raise as much as HK$14.54 billion ($1.9 billion) from selling new shares and convertible bonds, according to terms of the deals seen by Bloomberg News.

items.title: Airbus Employees in Spain Go on Strike Over Working Conditions
items.description: Airbus SE employees at a major manufacturing site in Spain have gone on strike in protest of what they say are deteriorating work conditions over the past years.

items.title: Trump Stops in UK to Switch Air Force One Planes
items.description:
President Trump’s return from the NATO summit in Turkey took a detour when he switched Air Force One planes in the UK.

items.title: Oil Edges Lower as Traders Assess Renewed US-Iran Hostilities

items.title: UBS Strategists Turn Biggest European Stock Bulls With 8% Upside
items.description: Stronger earnings and a broadening rally will power European stocks to new highs this year, according to UBS Group AG strategists, who are now the biggest bulls among forecasters tracked by Bloomberg.

items.title: Dronemaker Helsing Alters Staff Stock Plan Before Funding Round
items.description: European defense startup Helsing has overhauled its internal share program, alienating some staff as the company finalizes its biggest funding round to date.
